recipe.title: Potato gnocchi
recipe.description: A beautiful potato gnocchi recipe from Jamie Oliver. The best potatoes for gnocchi are floury potatoes, such as Maris Piper or King Edward. Total comfort food.

ingredients.name: floury potatoes
ingredients.note: such as Maris Piper, King Edward

steps.descr: Use potatoes of a fairly even size, as you’re going to...
steps.instructions: Use potatoes of a fairly even size, as you’re going to cook them whole. Cook in a large pan of boiling salted water for 20 to 25 minutes, or until tender.

on_enter.say: Use potatoes of a fairly even size, as you’re going to cook them whole. Cook in a large pan of boiling salted water for 20 to 25 minutes, or until tender.

steps.descr: Drain and leave until cool enough to handle, then remove...
steps.instructions: Drain and leave until cool enough to handle, then remove the skins.

on_enter.say: Drain and leave until cool enough to handle, then remove the skins.

steps.descr: Mash the potatoes using a potato ricer or masher on to a...
steps.instructions: Mash the potatoes using a potato ricer or masher on to a large clean board.

on_enter.say: Mash the potatoes using a potato ricer or masher on to a large clean board.

steps.descr: Sprinkle over the flour, finely grate over half the...
steps.instructions: Sprinkle over the flour, finely grate over half the nutmeg, season well with sea salt and black pepper, then scrunch and push the mixture together with clean hands. Depending on the potatoes, you may need to add a little more flour – use your common sense.

on_enter.say: Sprinkle over the flour, finely grate over half the nutmeg, season well with sea salt and black pepper, then scrunch and push the mixture together with clean hands. Depending on the potatoes, you may need to add a little more flour – use your common sense.

steps.descr: Knead on a flour-dusted surface for a few minutes until...
steps.instructions: Knead on a flour-dusted surface for a few minutes until pliable, then divide into 4 equal pieces and roll each into a sausage about 2cm thick.

on_enter.say: Knead on a flour-dusted surface for a few minutes until pliable, then divide into 4 equal pieces and roll each into a sausage about 2cm thick.

steps.descr: Slice into 3cm lengths – this is your basic gnocchi
steps.instructions: Slice into 3cm lengths – this is your basic gnocchi. Now you can gently roll each piece down a butter pat, the back of a fork or a fine grater to add grooves, which will help your sauce to stick, or simply roll into balls.

on_enter.say: Slice into 3cm lengths – this is your basic gnocchi. Now you can gently roll each piece down a butter pat, the back of a fork or a fine grater to add grooves, which will help your sauce to stick, or simply roll into balls.

steps.descr: Nonna Teresa cooked her gnocchi 2 portions at a time, as...
steps.instructions: Nonna Teresa cooked her gnocchi 2 portions at a time, as it’s so quick, and it also means they’re less likely to break up. Cook in a pan of boiling salted water for 2 to 3 minutes – as soon as they come up to the surface they’re ready.

on_enter.say: Nonna Teresa cooked her gnocchi 2 portions at a time, as it’s so quick, and it also means they’re less likely to break up. Cook in a pan of boiling salted water for 2 to 3 minutes – as soon as they come up to the surface they’re ready.

steps.descr: Remove with a slotted spoon and toss with melted butter...
steps.instructions: Remove with a slotted spoon and toss with melted butter and finely grated Parmesan, my Hero tomato sauce (or leftover sauce from a stew, which you can see in the picture), then finely grate over a little Parmesan, to serve. I also like to add a little dollop of pesto – homemade or jarred, it’s up to you.

on_enter.say: Remove with a slotted spoon and toss with melted butter and finely grated Parmesan, my Hero tomato sauce (or leftover sauce from a stew, which you can see in the picture), then finely grate over a little Parmesan, to serve. I also like to add a little dollop of pesto – homemade or jarred, it’s up to you.
